body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
irrlicht_observes: yes

## Reasoning

multi-turn-conversation resolves to coverage cell `basic-turn` (pi: yes/yes), exercising the same ready→working→ready turn-end detection but across THREE consecutive user prompts in ONE live session rather than a single headless round-trip. Pi supports this natively: an interactive pi REPL holds one rollout session open while accepting multiple prompts, writing each turn to the same JSONL file under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<UUID>.jsonl (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy). The pi parser maps each assistant message's stopReason=stop to turn_done, so the daemon observes the working→ready settle independently for each of the three turns on the SAME session_id, with state resetting to ready between prompts.

Unlike basic-turn (headless `pi --print`, single-shot, exits with process_exited after one reply), multi-turn requires the interactive driver (drive-pi-interactive.sh) to keep the tmux REPL alive across all three send+wait_turn cycles, then kill the session once — producing a SINGLE process_exited for the long-lived rollout session, not three. This is the structural difference the recording captures: three working→ready arcs and one teardown on one session_id.

irrlicht observes all of it: transcript_new (born ready), pid_discovered bound to the rollout session, three state_transition working/ready arcs, and one process_exited at REPL teardown. A transient proc-<PID> pre-session may appear at launch before the UUID transcript materializes; the spec binds session_birth (and all later phases via same_session_as) to the UUID rollout session so the pre-session can't satisfy a turn match.
caveats: Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID — it generates one itself. The interactive driver discovers the UUID from the session header on line 1 of the freshly-created rollout transcript.

The live REPL may briefly emit a proc-<PID> pre-session born ready before the UUID-keyed rollout transcript appears; the spec anchors session_birth to the UUID rollout session and chains every later phase off it via same_session_as.
3s sleeps between wait_turn and the next send sit past the classifier's debounce window so each turn's working→ready flip is observed cleanly before the next prompt re-enters working.

sources.note: Identity.Name=pi, Source variant FilesUnderRoot under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, ExactName{Name: "pi"} process matcher.
stopReason=stop on each assistant turn maps to turn_done (working→ready); three turns on one session yield three arcs.
Interactive tmux REPL driver supporting send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep; keeps one pi session alive across all three turns, then kills the tmux session once → single process_exited.
Sibling cell sharing coverage_id basic-turn (yes/yes); multi-turn extends it from one headless turn to three live turns on one session.
